world.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Previously Admitted to 2 Alcohol-Related Arrests
world.description: FBI Director Kash Patel is suing over claims he's been drunk on the job ... but he reportedly admitted in the past to twice being arrested for alcohol-related offenses around 2 decades ago.

world.title: ‘Powerhouse’ journalist Jim McDowell remembered after death aged 76
world.description: McDowell was northern editor of the Sunday World for 25 years.

world.title: Body of 1 missing Florida student found, roommate taken into custody

world.title: Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff to head to Islamabad for direct talks after Iran

business.title: IEA’s Birol Says Iran War Will Permanently Cut Into Future Oil Demand
business.description: International Energy Agency chief Fatih Birol says the Iran war has permanently changed the fossil fuel industry and will accelerate a shift toward renewables, nuclear power and electrification at the expense of oil demand.

business.title: White House mulls using Defense Production Act in Spirit Airlines takeover

business.title: Stocks Hit All-Time Highs on US-Iran Talk Optimism: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- Hopes the US and Iran will be back to the negotiating table drove stocks to all-time highs, with the market also rising after a blowout forecast from Intel Corp. and the closing of a probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

business.title: Led Cycle Signals Delayed Surge as $2,300 Test Eyes Immediate Breakout
business.description: A tightening price structure is drawing fresh attention to Ethereum price action, as ETH trades near a key resistance zone following months of subdued momentum.

business.title: Fuel price concerns drive EV demand, share in new car sales up at 5.1% in March
business.description: Indian car buyers are shifting towards electric vehicles. Concerns over fuel availability and price hikes due to the West Asia conflict are driving this trend. Sales of electric cars have seen a significant jump. Industry leaders note a durable structural shift. Companies are expanding their electric vehicle offerings and charging infrastructure to meet growing demand.

business.title: This is a ‘come to Jesus moment’: Ford CEO says American carmakers are battling a perfect storm

science.title: AI Learns to Predict Breast Cancer Risk from How Single Cells Respond to Pressure
science.description: Findings support model in which accelerated biological aging of mammary epithelia may underpin breast cancer susceptibility across genetic and non-genetic risk groups.

science.title: Astronaut explains profound existential pain after moon orbit
science.description: Seeing the Earth from space gives people an entirely new perspective in life. It can be exhilarating and isolating.

science.title: Colibre: A New Cosmic Simulation With Cinematic Flair
science.description: The new Colibre cosmological simulation includes more critical detail than previous simulations. It also includes updated models of things like AGN feedback and star formation. The simulations also include a sonic component, giving the results a cinematic and information-rich flair.

science.title: Meet X1: First multi-robot team used for rescue missions
science.description: X-1 robotic system uses humanoids and drones, combining land and air abilities, to tackle rescue missions too dangerous for humans.

science.title: Utah Startup Claims Its Lab-Grown Sperm Can Produce Embryos, in Potential Fertility Breakthrough
science.description: Paterna Biosciences said it has mastered the molecular signals that teach stem cells how to mature into sperm for a promising new fertility treatment.

science.title: Pen ink found inside Martian meteorites, exposing a larger issue

science.title: An asteroid may hold the key to cutting Mars travel times far more than anyone expected

ai.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge

ai.title: 200 Judges Have Signed Up To Learn AI-The Other 31,500 Should Too
ai.description: The Judicial AI Consortium is free, judges-only, and run by three sitting judges. About 200 have joined. The evidence landing in courtrooms says that is not enough.

ai.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman 'deeply sorry' over Tumbler Ridge shooting
ai.description: SAN FRANCISCO — The head of OpenAI says he is deeply sorry that his company didn't alert law enforcement to worrisome online behavior of the person in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., before she shot and killed eight people earlier this year.

ai.title: Data centers are finding a surprising way to deploy batteries
ai.description: As AI strains the grid, data center operators are pairing batteries with natural gas generators to keep power flowing, turning a key clean-energy technology into a tool for fossil-fuel backup.
